what is a silver core

A silver core is like a soul or soul container yes. The awakening process uses that as the core to build/recreate a body out of thematically appropriate materials.
Most people leave behind little to no silver when dissoluted, some people leave behind a silver core
Also not all awakeners are dead, some are just straight up supernatural beings, or victims of experimentation or uh .. isekaied from a dead gacha game
It doesn't really prevent dissolution - most awakeners already died to it, and a silver core can survive a long time without issue
As for how aegis is still remembered - dissolution doesn't immediately erase someone from memory, it seems to be a slow process
it also varies from person to person. some people might remember a dissoluted individual longer than others
and i think being awakened halts that process
that i have no confirmation in either direction, so let's say it holds true for now
i guess the exception would be what happened to castor, but thats special circumstances

so i skipped the prologue and the aigis chapter story i only started reading seriously after that but bc of that im also missing some details for the setting and stuff could someone give me a tldr of the early story and setting?
i was today years old when i found out :

Prologue: your graduation ceremony in becoming a Keeper gets jumped by dissolution. You gather the gang and go to the basement of the school and fight Tawil (who looks like Ramona, hhhhhmmmmm).

Ch1: you get sent as your first assignment as a keeper to investigate disappearence of people, later finding out they are being sent to a lab for experiments along with the poor kids, one of them being Aigis resulting in... The monster fight. Aigis natural properties along with the experiments took its toll and she perishes, but thanks to the orphanage director, she was able to retrieve Aigis's Silver core. 

Which the keeper uses to revive her via the black pool (which is under the school)
